title: calandrinia sericea var. sessiliflora
intro: Calandrinia sericea var. sessiliflora, also known as the silky pink purslane, is a variety of flowering plant in the portulacaceae family, characterized by its pink to reddish flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Calandrinia sericea var. sessiliflora include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and potentially aiding in the management of certain skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for medicinal use.
